Time Certain - Land Use - To Be Heard No Earlier Than 10:00 a.m.
8. Public Hearing - Appeal of the December 18, 2025 decision of the Planning Commission to deny a concurrent land use application. (Department of Planning and Development)
a. Public hearing to consider appeal of the December 18, 2025, decision of the Planning Commission to uphold the Zoning Administrator’s decision to deny a concurrent land use application, consisting of Design Review and Grading Approval. Appellant: Jeff and Melissa Waters. Applicant: Cove Britton. Owner: The Waters Family Trust. Property Address/Location: 0 Peacock Court, Cupertino, Assessor's Parcel No. 351-42-004. Zoning: HS-d1. Supervisorial District: Five. File No.: PLN20-124-APL3. (LA)
i. Open public hearing and receive testimony.
ii. Close public hearing.
Hearing Printout
a. Copy of Notice
b. Affidavit of Mailing
c. Public CommentSupplemental Information
d. Presentation from Nossaman, LLC relating to Appeal of Planning Commission December 18, 2025 Decision regarding Concurrent Land Use ApplicationSupplemental Information
e. Presentation from Matson Britton Architects relating to Appeal of Planning Commission December 18, 2025 Decision regarding Concurrent Land Use ApplicationSupplemental Information
f. Public Comment No. 2
g. Public Comment No. 3
b. Consider recommendations relating to the appeal of the December 18, 2025 decision by the Planning Commission to uphold the Zoning Administrator’s decision to deny a concurrent land use application, consisting of Design Review and Grading Approval that includes construction of a new 9,200-square-foot single-family residence with attached garages, a detached accessory dwelling unit with an attached indoor basketball court, and site improvements including a driveway and septic system. (LA)
Possible action:
i. Declare intent to deny the appeal of Jeff and Melissa Waters of the decision of the Planning Commission to deny the application for concurrent land use for Design Review and Grading Approval for the property located at 0 Peacock Court, Cupertino, and refer to County Counsel for preparation of findings. California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA): Not applicable pursuant to Public Resources Code Section 21080(b)(5) and Section 15270 of the CEQA Guidelines.
OR
ii. Declare intent to grant the appeal of Jeff and Melissa Waters of the decision of the Planning Commission to deny the application for concurrent land use for Design Review and Grading Approval, for the property located at 0 Peacock Court, Cupertino, and refer to County Counsel for preparation of findings. California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA): Exempt pursuant to Section 15303 (Class 3a) of the CEQA Guidelines.
Land Use Printout
a. Attachment A - Appellant's Statement for January 2, 2025 Appeal (bracketed) (1)
b. Attachment B - Staff Response to Appeal Items (bracketed)
c. Attachment C - 12.18.2025 Planning Commission Transcript
d. Attachment D - Project Plans Submitted on September 16, 2024 and July 3, 2024
e. Attachment E - Design Review and Grading Findings (Updated September 2024)
f. Attachment F - Alternate Packet
g. Attachment G - Policy and Findings Matrix
h. Attachment H – Guidelines for Grading and Hillside Development
i. Attachment I – Design Review Guidelines
j. Attachment J - Water Will-Serve Letter and Environmental Health Persisting Issues with Water Supply Compliance
k. Attachment K – Neighborhood Development Data
l. Attachment L - GIS Reverse Viewshed Analysis, and Site Photos
m. Attachment M - Staff Report and Attachments for the November 4, 2021 ZA Hearing
n. Attachment N - Zoning Administrator Denial Letter
o. Attachment O - Appellant's Statement for November 19, 2021 Appeal
p. Attachment P - Timeline and Correspondences Regarding Resubmittal After the Project was Deemed Complete
q. Attachment Q – Timeline and Letters Emails After the February 24, 2022 Appeal Hearing
r. Supplemental Memorandum from Department of Planning and Development relating to Appeal of the Planning Commission Decision of Peacock CourtSupplemental Information
Time Certain - To Be Heard No Earlier Than 10:00 a.m.
9. Public Hearing - Purchase of real property located at 21300 Almaden Road. (Parks and Recreation Department)
a. Public hearing to consider approval of purchase of real property for a public park purpose located at 21300 Almaden Road, in unincorporated Santa Clara County, California, known as Assessor's Parcel No. 583-18-045.
i. Open public hearing and receive testimony.
ii. Close public hearing.
Hearing Printout
a. Copy of Notice - 21300 Almaden Rd
b. BOS-2026-66 : Adopt Resolution to consummate purchase of real property located at 21300 Almaden Road, in unincorporated Santa Clara County, Assessor's Parcel No. 583-18-045; authorizing the President of the Board of Supervisors to execute the Purchase and Sale Agreement; making related findings; and delegating authority to the County Executive, or designee, to execute all documents and agreements necessary to consummate the purchase, following approval by County Counsel as to form and legality, and approval by the Office of the County Executive. California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA): Exempt from CEQA pursuant to Sections 15316 and 15325 of the CEQA Guidelines. Delegation of authority shall expire on June 30, 2027. (LA)
Resolution Printout
a. Purchase and Sale Agreement - New Almaden Post Office Property
b. Resolution to Consummate Purchase New Almaden Post Office
c. New Almaden Post Office Parcel Map
Board Referrals
10. Held from April 28, 2026 (Item No. 10): Approve referral to County Counsel, in coordination with Administration, to report to the Board with options for consideration relating to land use regulations and cost recovery reimbursement mechanisms to support the installation of solar agrivoltaics at Agricultural-Large Scale parcels. (Lee)
Board Referral Printout
a. Proposed Referral Cover Sheet - Solar Agrivoltaics
b. Public CommentSupplemental Information
c. Public Comment No. 2Supplemental Information
d. Public Comment No. 3Supplemental Information
e. Presentation relating to Solar Agrivoltaics Technology
f. Public Comment No. 4
g. Revised Presentation relating to Solar Agrivoltaics Technology Information
h. Public Comment No. 5
11. Approve referral to Administration to schedule a study session on child welfare in conjunction with the quarterly update on child welfare reforms, currently scheduled for August 25, 2026, to include input and feedback from external experts and professionals at the state and national level, including from the California Department of Social Services, academia, and professionals who work directly with children and youth in the child welfare system; evidence-based practices that are currently being implemented from comparable counties across California (or the United States) that are focused and centered on keeping children safe as well as thriving in their relationships with their family and cultural background; and, information on child welfare trends since 2015, including available demographic and child welfare history information for Santa Clara County and statewide. (Ellenberg/Lee)
Board Referral Printout
a. Proposed Referral Cover Sheet re Child Welfare Best Practices Study Session

Adoption of Salary Ordinance No. NS-5.26.78, an Ordinance amending Santa Clara County Salary Ordinance No. NS-5.26 relating to compensation of employees adding one limited-term Social Worker II or Social Worker I position in the Social Services Agency. Position shall expire at 11:59 p.m., on June 30, 2028.

66. Consider recommendations relating to the renewal of service agreements for Office of Diversion and Reentry Services for Fiscal Year 2026-2027. (LA)
Possible action:
a. Approve First Amendment to Agreement with Mission Services of Silicon Valley, LLC, a division of Goodwill of Silicon Valley, relating to providing job readiness and employment services programming for justice-involved adults, increasing the maximum contract amount by $1,932,385 from $1,932,385 to $3,864,770, and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality.
b. Approve Eighth Amendment to Agreement with Bible Way Christian Center relating to providing faith-based reentry service navigation services, increasing the maximum contract amount by $400,000 from $2,387,500 to $2,787,500 and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality. An exception to Board of Supervisors Policy 5.4.5.4, Length of Term of Contracts, has been approved by the Office of Countywide Contracting Management.
c. Approve Eighth Amendment to Agreement with Breakout Prison Outreach, dba California Youth Outreach, relating to providing faith-based reentry service navigation services, increasing the maximum contract amount by $400,000 from $2,387,500 to $2,787,500, and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality. An exception to Board of Supervisors Policy 5.4.5.4, Length of Term of Contracts, has been approved by the Office of Countywide Contracting Management.
d. Approve Ninth Amendment to Agreement with Catholic Charities of Santa Clara County relating to providing faith-based reentry service navigation services, increasing the maximum contract amount by $400,000 from $2,387,500 to $2,787,500, and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality. An exception to Board of Supervisors Policy 5.4.5.4, Length of Term of Contracts, has been approved by the Office of Countywide Contracting Management.
e. Approve Eighth Amendment to Agreement with Bay Area Maranatha Christian Center relating to providing faith-based reentry service navigation services, increasing the maximum contract amount by $400,000 from $2,387,500 to $2,787,500 and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality. An exception to Board of Supervisors Policy 5.4.5.4, Length of Term of Contracts, has been approved by the Office of Countywide Contracting Management.
f. Approve First Amendment to Agreement with Breakout Prison Outreach, dba California Youth Outreach, relating to providing reentry service navigation support services, increasing the maximum contract amount by $463,608 from $622,721 to $1,086,329, and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality.
g. Approve Seventh Amendment to Agreement with Carry the Vision relating to providing life skills programming to justice-involved adults, increasing the maximum contract amount by $335,000 from $1,911,942 to $2,246,942, and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality. An exception to Board of Supervisors Policy 5.4.5.4, Length of Term of Contracts, has been approved by the Office of Countywide Contracting Management.
h. Approve Third Amendment to Agreement with Douglas Parking, LLC, relating to providing a shuttle service program for individuals released from County correctional facilities, clients of the Office of Diversion and Reentry Services, and other County partners who serve justice-involved populations, increasing the maximum contract amount by $209,849 from $677,674 to $887,523, and extending the agreement for a 12-month period through June 30, 2027, that has been reviewed and approved by County Counsel as to form and legality.

b. Approve the Project, adopt plans and specifications, authorize advertisement of Contract Documents for the SCVMC Building M OR Renovation project (Project No. 263-CP23008) and direct the Clerk of the Board to open bids on Thursday, September 17, 2026 at 2:00 p.m., in the Office of the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors. Final Adoption of Ordinances - Central Fire Protection District
158. Adoption of Salary Ordinance No. CFPD-2026-2-O, an Ordinance amending Santa Clara County Central Fire Protection District Salary Ordinance No. CFPD-2026-1-O relating to compensation of employees, deleting the classifications of Database Administrator, Information Specialist, Principal Financial Analyst/Accountant, and Senior Management Analyst and adding the classifications of Deputy Director of Business Services, Deputy Director of Personnel Services, and Public Information Specialist; deleting one Community Risk Program Director position, one Database Administrator position, one Deputy Fire Marshal I or Deputy Fire Marshal II or Associate Fire Plans Examiner or Fire Plans Examiner position, one Information Specialist position, one Principal Financial Analyst/Accountant position, one Senior Community Risk Specialist position, and one Senior Management Analyst position; adding one Battalion Chief or Community Risk Program Director position, one Community Risk Specialist I or Community Risk Specialist II position, one Deputy Director of Business Services position, one Deputy Director of Personnel Services position, 14 Fire Captain positions, 20 Firefighter/Engineer or Firefighter or extra-help Firefighter Trainee positions, one General Maintenance Craftworker position, one Parts and Service Supervisor position, one Public Information Specialist position, one Senior Deputy Fire Marshal position, and one Senior Systems Analyst position; and, deleting 20 overfill positions.

              •              Introduce and preliminarily adopt on June 2, 2026.

              •              Adopt (Final) on June 23, 2026.
